title: Wasp Removal in Irvington, NJ | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional wasp removal services in Irvington, NJ. Safe nest elimination from homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call now for $350-$550 wasp control solutions.
intro: Dealing with aggressive wasps in Irvington, NJ? Best Pest Control NYC provides fast, effective wasp removal services to protect your home or business from dangerous nests. Our experienced technicians safely eliminate wasp colonies from properties throughout Irvington, ensuring your family and customers can enjoy outdoor spaces without fear of stings.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Irvington residents for years with reliable, same-day wasp removal services. Our licensed technicians use proven methods to locate and eliminate nests while prioritizing the safety of your property and loved ones.
localContext: From the historic Victorian homes near Clinton Avenue to modern apartment complexes and the commercial buildings along Springfield Avenue, Irvington properties face wasp infestations in eaves, attics, and outdoor structures. The tree-lined neighborhoods near Irvington Park provide ideal nesting conditions for yellow jackets, paper wasps, and hornets throughout the warmer months.

faqs.question: How much does wasp removal cost in Irvington, NJ?
faqs.answer: Our wasp removal services in Irvington typically range from $350 to $550, depending on the nest location, size, and accessibility. We provide upfront pricing after a thorough inspection of your property, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a wasp nest in Irvington?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and emergency wasp removal services throughout Irvington. Most wasp nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, and our technicians can typically arrive at your property within a few hours of your call during peak season.

faqs.question: What types of wasps are common in Irvington, NJ?
faqs.answer: Irvington properties commonly encounter yellow jackets, paper wasps, bald-faced hornets, and European hornets. These species build nests in attics, under eaves, in wall voids, trees, and underground, becoming increasingly aggressive during late summer and early fall.

faqs.question: Is wasp removal safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our certified technicians use EPA-approved treatments and follow strict safety protocols during wasp removal. We advise keeping children and pets indoors during treatment, and the area is typically safe to access within a few hours after service completion.
